1	# AGENTS.md — Designflow Backend: Primary Developer Guide
2	
3	Read this file in full before touching any code. It is the authoritative orientation document for any developer or AI session working on this repository.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. What this service is
8	
9	Designflow Backend is the main REST API for the Designflow PLM platform. It is a stateless Node.js + Express service backed by PostgreSQL (Sequelize ORM). Identity comes from a JWT on every request; session state does not exist. The service runs on Google Cloud Run and is reached exclusively through the BFF (`designflow-bff`), which terminates the user JWT and adds an OIDC service-to-service token before forwarding. The frontend is a separate Angular SPA in a sibling repository. For a full system diagram see `docs/architecture.md`.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 2. Multi-model note
14	
15	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
16	
17	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
18	
19	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the "What to ignore" section below.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## 3. Repository structure
24	
25	| Path | What it is |
26	|---|---|
27	| `index.js` | Server entry — middleware stack (including the `x-user-authorization` → `authorization` swap) and `listen` |
28	| `config/env.js` | dotenv loader; resolves `.env.${NODE_ENV}` |
29	| `config/db.config.js` | PostgreSQL connection params; throws on missing required key |
30	| `config/digitalocean.config.js` | DigitalOcean Spaces credentials |
31	| `config/brevo.config.js` | Brevo email credentials |
32	| `helpers/auth.js` | JWT RS256 verify; `authRole(roles[])` middleware factory; `getUserInfo(token)` |
33	| `helpers/context.middleware.js` | `AsyncLocalStorage` per-request store `{ user, reqId }` — lets deep-stack code read the current user without prop-drilling |
34	| `helpers/openrouter.js` | OpenRouter API client; prompt-caching markers for anthropic/deepseek/google models |
35	| `helpers/deepseek.js` | DeepSeek direct API client (`api.deepseek.com`); maps `reasoning_effort: 'none'\|'high'\|'max'` to the DeepSeek `thinking` parameter; used when `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` is set and `api='deepseek-direct'` |
36	| `helpers/section301.js` | Section 301 PDF text extraction + regex HTS parser (lazy-requires `pdf-parse`) |
37	| `helpers/hts-rates.js` | MFN base-rate lookup against bundled USITC HTS JSON (`data/hts-rates.json`) |
38	| `helpers/trump2025.js` | Trump 2025 reciprocal-tariff lookup; alias-aware country normalization |
39	| `helpers/factories.js` | Factory list / country map / `updateCountry(id, country)` |
40	| `helpers/digitalocean.js` | S3 client; presigned URL, ACL, upload, delete; `uploadBufferToSpaces(buffer, fileName, contentType)` for in-memory Buffer uploads (used by bulk import to avoid writing temp files) |
41	| `helpers/mention.js` | `parseMentions` / `resolveMentions` for @mention pipeline |
42	| `helpers/multer.js` | Multer instances: `multerGetThumb` (disk, thumbnails), `multerDigitalOcean` (disk, general uploads), `multerMemory` (memory storage, 25 MB limit — xlsx bulk imports) |
43	| `helpers/errorHandler.js` | Express error-handler middleware |
44	| `helpers/quote-access.js` | `vendorMayModifyQuote(userInfo, rfqVendor)` — ownership guard preventing a vendor from mutating another factory's quote by `quote_id` (see §11) |
45	| `helpers/rfqBulkImport.js` | Factory bulk-offer xlsx workflow: `generateTemplate(vendorName, customers)` → ExcelJS workbook buffer; `parseImport(buffer)` → `{rows, images}`; `getNextFoffPriceCodes(sql, count, Op)` → sequential `foff-XXXX` price code array |
46	| `helpers/rfqCopyMerchGroupGuard.js` | Guard: prevents copying an RFQ item into a closed/inactive merch group (uses `is_active` flag) |
47	| `helpers/rfqHistory.js` | Copy-lineage traversal via `rfqItem_copied_from_id`; used by `getRfqItemHistory` endpoint |
48	| `helpers/rfqStepDedupe.js` | Deduplication helper for RFQ step-change records |
49	| `helpers/utility.js` | Shared utility functions |
50	| `models/db.js` | Sequelize init + model registration + **all schema migrations** (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`) |
51	| `models/db/` | 60+ Sequelize model files (one per table) |
52	| `models/lib.model.js` | Legacy domain-method module (`Customer` namespace — see §11) |
53	| `routes/index.js` | Root router — mounts all subrouters |
54	| `routes/lib.router.js` | **150+ endpoints** mounted at `/` — RFQ, vendor, product CRUD. Never read whole — grep or use offset/limit |
55	| `routes/admin.router.js` | `/api/admin` — app settings, section 301, HTS rates, Trump 2025, factories, impersonation |
56	| `routes/auth.router.js` | `/auth` — Azure SSO login |
57	| `routes/notification.router.js` | `/api/notifications` |
58	| `routes/user.router.js` | `/api/user` — profile, photo upload |
59	| `routes/openrouter.router.js` | `/api/openrouter` — AI proxy |
60	| `routes/cross.router.js` | `/api/cross` — CBP CROSS rulings proxy |
61	| `routes/comment.router.js` | `/api/comment` |
62	| `routes/customer.router.js` | `/api/customers` |
63	| `routes/grid.router.js` | `/api/grid` |
64	| `routes/health.router.js` | `/api/health` |
65	| `routes/digitalocean.router.js` | File storage endpoints mounted at `/` |
66	| `routes/` (others) | `itemSize`, `itemDepth`, `std`, `role`, `autofill` |
67	| `services/` | Business logic layer — Sequelize queries and domain operations |
68	| `controllers/` | Thin route handlers; delegate to services or `models/lib.model.js` |
69	| `tests/unit/` | Jest unit tests (227 tests across 19 suites, hermetic, runs in ~1 s) |
70	| `data/hts-rates.json` | Bundled USITC HTS schedule (~1 MB, ~13.6 k codes) — regenerated yearly by `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` |
71	| `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` | One-shot downloader to rebuild `data/hts-rates.json` from USITC |
72	| `docs/` | `architecture.md`, `configuration.md`, `development.md`, `deployment.md` |
73	| `.env.example` | Template for local `.env.<env-name>` files |
74	| `Dockerfile` | Single-stage Node production image |
75	| `cloudbuild.yaml` | Google Cloud Build CI/CD definition |
76	| `node_modules/` | Third-party packages — **never edit** |
77	
78	---
79	
80	## 4. The Prime Directive
81	
82	The entire repo is custom code — there is no committed vendor framework directory analogous to the frontend's `src/@vex/`. Everything under the repo root is fair game **except**:
83	
84	- `node_modules/` — never edit.
85	- `models/db/*.js` model files are effectively schema declarations. When you add a column to a model, you **must** also add the matching `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migration in `models/db.js` or the column will never exist in production. See §6 Decision Tree.
86	- `routes/lib.router.js` and `models/lib.model.js` are giant legacy files (150+ endpoints, ~3000 lines). **Add to them surgically rather than refactoring.** Any refactor that touches most of the file needs a coordinated effort — not a solo AI session.
87	
88	---
89	
90	## 5. Core modification inventory

92	No third-party or framework files outside our directories have been intentionally modified. There is no vendor layer to track here.

content.content:
1	# Architecture
2	
3	## System overview
4	
5	Node.js / Express HTTP API (CommonJS). Stateless — identity comes from the JWT on every request. PostgreSQL is the only persistent store; DigitalOcean Spaces holds uploaded files. No in-memory session state.
6	
7	```
8	Browser / Frontend SPA
9	  └── BFF (designflow-bff, port 5004)
10	        └── Core backend (this repo, port 5000)
11	              ├── PostgreSQL (Sequelize ORM)
12	              └── DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible file storage)
13	
14	External outbound calls:
15	  POST https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/...    ← AI classification proxy
16	  GET  https://rulings.cbp.gov/api/search  ← CBP CROSS rulings proxy
17	  POST https://api.brevo.com/v3/...        ← transactional email
18	  GET  https://login.microsoftonline.com/  ← Azure JWKS (key cache 10 min)
19	```
20	
21	---
22	
23	## Project layout
24	
25	```
26	index.js                    Server entry: middleware wiring, listen
27	config/
28	  env.js                    Resolves .env.${NODE_ENV} filename, loads dotenv
29	  db.config.js              PostgreSQL connection params (throws on missing key)
30	  digitalocean.config.js    DO Spaces credentials from env
31	  brevo.config.js           Brevo email credentials from env
32	helpers/
33	  auth.js                   JWT RS256 verify; authRole(roles[]) middleware factory; getUserInfo(token)
34	  context.middleware.js     AsyncLocalStorage per-request context: { user, reqId }
35	  digitalocean.js           S3 client; getPreSignedUrl, setObjectPublic, uploadObjectsToBucket, deleteObjectsFromBucket, setCORSForBucket
36	  openrouter.js             OpenRouter API client: listUserModels, chatCompletion (with prompt-caching markers)
37	  cross.js                  CBP CROSS rulings proxy
38	  section301.js             Section 301 PDF text extraction + regex HTS parser (lazy-requires pdf-parse)
39	  hts-rates.js              MFN base-rate lookup against bundled USITC HTS JSON (data/hts-rates.json)
40	  trump2025.js              Trump 2025 reciprocal-tariff country-rate lookup (alias-aware)
41	  factories.js              Factory list / country map / update-country accessors
42	  mention.js                parseMentions(text) → @handles; resolveMentions(handles, users) → IDs
43	  multer.js                 Multer instances (memory storage) configured per upload type
44	  imageMagick.service.js    ImageMagick shell wrapper for image processing
45	  jimp.service.js           Jimp-based image resize/thumbnail
46	  brevo.js                  Brevo email send wrapper
47	  utility.js                Shared utility functions
48	  errorHandler.js           Express error-handler middleware
49	data/
50	  hts-rates.json            ~1 MB compact USITC HTS export: { "NNNN.NN.NN": { d, g } } (13.6k codes)
51	scripts/
52	  fetch-hts-rates.js        One-shot downloader that rebuilds data/hts-rates.json from
53	                            https://hts.usitc.gov/reststop/exportList — re-run yearly when
54	                            USITC publishes a new revision
55	models/
56	  db.js                     Sequelize init + model registration + safe migrations (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS;
57	                            mixed-case identifiers MUST be double-quoted — see Inline migrations below)
58	  db/                       60+ Sequelize model files
59	routes/
60	  index.js                  Root router — mounts all subrouters (see Routes below)
61	  lib.router.js             Mounted at /  — RFQ, product, vendor CRUD (150+ endpoints)
62	  digitalocean.router.js    Mounted at /  — file storage endpoints
63	  auth.router.js            Mounted at /auth
64	  admin.router.js           Mounted at /api/admin
65	  notification.router.js    Mounted at /api/notifications
66	  user.router.js            Mounted at /api/user
67	  openrouter.router.js      Mounted at /api/openrouter
68	  cross.router.js           Mounted at /api/cross
69	  comment.router.js         Mounted at /api/comment
70	  customer.router.js        Mounted at /api/customers
71	  grid.router.js            Mounted at /api/grid
72	  health.router.js          Mounted at /api
73	  ... (itemSize, itemDepth, std, role, autofill)
74	services/                   Business logic layer (Sequelize queries, domain operations)
75	controllers/                Route handler functions (thin — delegate to services)
76	tests/unit/                 Jest unit tests
77	```
78	
79	---
80	
81	## Routes
82	
83	### Health
84	- `GET /api/health` → `{ status: 'ok', message, timestamp }`
85	
86	### Authentication
87	- `POST /auth/azure` — exchange Azure AD ID token for Designflow JWT; auto-provisions new users (unauthenticated)
88	
89	### User
90	- `GET  /api/user/me` — current user profile `{ firstName, name, email, photoUrl }` (all roles). Source table depends on the JWT `role`: staff resolve from `users` by `id`; `vendor` (factory) users resolve from the `vendor` table by `vendor_id` (see AGENTS.md §11). Returns 404 only if no matching row exists.
91	- `POST /api/user/photo` — upload profile avatar (multipart `photo`, max 5 MB, all roles). Stored on DO Spaces under `profile-photos/user-<id>` or `profile-photos/vendor-<id>` by role; URL persisted to `users.profile_photo` or `vendor.vendor_profile_photo`.
92	
93	### Notifications
94	- `GET  /api/notifications` — unread notifications for current user (most recent 20, all roles)
95	- `GET  /api/notifications/count` — unread count for current user (all roles)
96	- `GET  /api/notifications/mentionable-users` — active users for @mention autocomplete (all roles)
97	- `POST /api/notifications/create` — create notification for a specific user (all roles)
98	- `PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read` — mark one notification read (all roles)
99	- `POST /api/notifications/read-all` — mark all notifications read (all roles)
100	- `DELETE /api/notifications` — permanently delete all notifications for current user (all roles)
101	
102	### Admin
103	- `GET  /api/admin/app-settings/:key` — read setting (admin)
104	- `PUT  /api/admin/app-settings/:key` — write setting (admin)
105	- `POST /api/admin/section301/upload` — parse USTR Section 301 PDF; store HTS lookup (admin). Legacy — UI-hidden but still wired.
106	- `POST /api/admin/section301/upload-csv` — parse USTR Section 301 CSV (preferred over PDF); same `app_settings.section301_lookup` storage (admin)
107	- `GET  /api/admin/section301/status` — `{ loaded, codesFound, lists }` — no full payload (admin, sourcing_manager)
108	- `GET  /api/admin/section301/lookup?hts=` — look up one HTS code (admin, sourcing_manager)
109	- `GET  /api/admin/hts-rates/status` — `{ loaded, codesFound }` for the bundled USITC schedule (admin, sourcing_manager, designer)
110	- `GET  /api/admin/hts-rates/lookup?hts=` — MFN general rate by HTS code (10→8→6-digit prefix fallback)
111	- `GET  /api/admin/trump2025/list` — Trump 2025 country → rate list (admin, sourcing_manager, designer)
112	- `PUT  /api/admin/trump2025/list` — replace the full list (admin only)
113	- `GET  /api/admin/trump2025/lookup?country=` — alias-aware case-insensitive rate lookup
114	- `GET  /api/admin/factories` — full factory list with `factory_country` (admin, sourcing_manager)
115	- `GET  /api/admin/factories/countries` — compact `{ factory_nickname: factory_country }` map (all RFQ-viewing roles)
116	- `PATCH /api/admin/factories/:id` — update one factory's `factory_country` (admin, sourcing_manager)
117	- `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId` — issue 2-hour impersonation JWT for a normal user, `{ targetType: 'factory' }` visible factory row, or `{ targetType: 'vendor' }` factory rep (admin)
118	- Art types, artist types, artists, age groups, merch groups CRUD
119	
120	### AI / OpenRouter proxy
121	- `GET  /api/openrouter/models` — proxies GET /api/v1/models/user from openrouter.ai (API key required, no route auth)
122	- `POST /api/openrouter/chat` — proxies chat completions (API key required, no route auth)
123	
124	### CBP CROSS
125	- `GET  /api/cross/search?term=&pageSize=10&page=1` — proxies CBP CROSS ruling search (no auth)
126	
127	### File storage (mounted at root `/`)
128	- `POST /getPreSignedUrl` — generate S3 presigned PUT URL for direct browser upload (15 min expiry)
129	- `POST /setObjectPublic` — apply `public-read` ACL to an uploaded object; returns `{ publicUrl }` — called by frontend after upload to make the object publicly accessible
130	- `POST /saveTechLink/:id` — save a DO Spaces file URL to an item record
131	- `POST /deleteBucketObjects` — delete files from the bucket
132	- `GET  /getListOfBuckets`, `POST /getBucketObjects`, `PUT /setCORSForBucket` — bucket admin
133	
134	### RFQ / product grid
135	- `POST /api/grid/getAllItem`, `getGridHeader`, `saveGridLayout`, `getCustomGridLayout`
136	- Vendor quotes (in `routes/lib.router.js`): `POST /updateVendor`, `/sendPriceReq`, `/addingVendor`. **Re-quote (stale price refresh):** `POST /requestRequote` (internal roles only — flags a factory's quote and emails the factory for a fresh price) and `POST /keepPrice` (vendor reconfirms the current price, clearing the flag). Both resolve the quote by `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) and run through the `vendorMayModifyQuote` ownership guard. See `models/lib.model.js` `Customer.requestRequote` / `Customer.keepPrice`.
137	- **Archive:** `POST /archiveRFQItem` archives/un-archives an item. For internal roles it toggles the global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive`; for vendors it toggles a **per-vendor** `RFQVendor_archived` on their own quote row (so it never hides the item from Sourcing or other factories). On a vendor's list load, `Customer.getAll` auto-archives that factory's priced quotes older than 3 months (`helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js`). A vendor un-archive is sticky (`RFQVendor_archive_optout`); `requestRequote`, a fresh price, and `keepPrice` auto-un-archive / reset the cycle. See AGENTS.md §11.
138	
139	### Other
140	- `/api/customers/**`, `/api/item-size/**`, `/api/item_depth/**`, `/api/comment/**`, `/api/role/**`, `/api/autofill/**`, `/std/**`
141	
142	---
143	
144	## Authentication
145	
146	All protected routes use `authRole(roles[])` from `helpers/auth.js`:
147	
148	1. Reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. For Cloud Run requests coming through the BFF, `index.js` first middleware swaps `x-user-authorization` → `authorization` (the BFF puts the OIDC token in `authorization` for Cloud Run; the user JWT travels in `x-user-authorization` and is restored before any route handler runs)
149	2. `jwt.verify(token, JWT_PUBLIC_KEY, { algorithms: ['RS256'] })`
150	3. Checks `decoded.role` against the allowed roles array

1	# Configuration
2	
3	## Environment files
4	
5	Config is loaded from `.env.${NODE_ENV}` by `config/env.js` using `dotenv`. `NODE_ENV` is set by the start script (`cross-env NODE_ENV=...`).
6	
7	In production (Cloud Run) there is no `.env` file — variables are injected by Cloud Build via `--set-secrets` (GCP Secret Manager) and `--set-env-vars`.
8	
9	Copy `.env.example` to create a local config file:
10	
11	```bash
12	cp .env.example .env.sandbox-albert
13	# fill in real values
14	```
15	
16	## Environment variables
17	
18	| Variable | Injected via | Purpose |
19	|----------|-------------|---------|
20	| `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME` | Secret Manager | PostgreSQL connection |
21	| `JWT_PRIVATE_KEY` | Secret Manager | RS256 JWT signing (RS private key PEM) |
22	| `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` | Secret Manager | RS256 JWT verification (RS public key PEM) |
23	| `DO_ACCESS_KEY`, `DO_SECRET_KEY` | Secret Manager | DigitalOcean Spaces credentials |
24	| `BREVO_API_KEY` | Secret Manager | Brevo transactional email |
25	| `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | Secret Manager | OpenRouter AI proxy |
26	| `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` | Secret Manager | DeepSeek direct AI API key (used by `helpers/deepseek.js`; throws at call time if absent) |
27	| `WEB_URL` | `--set-env-vars` | Sent as `HTTP-Referer` to OpenRouter; logged at startup |
28	| `API_ADDRESS` | `--set-env-vars` | Logged at startup (informational) |
29	| `SCHEMA` | `--set-env-vars` | PostgreSQL schema name (Sequelize `schema` option) |
30	| `DO_ENDPOINT_URL` | `--set-env-vars` | DigitalOcean Spaces endpoint (e.g. `https://sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`) |
31	| `DO_BUCKET_NAME` | `--set-env-vars` | Spaces bucket name |
32	| `DO_PUBLIC_URL` | `--set-env-vars` | Public base URL for serving bucket objects (e.g. `https://dflowbucket.sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com`) — returned by `POST /setObjectPublic` |
33	| `BREVO_BASE_URL` | `--set-env-vars` | Brevo API base URL |
34	| `BREVO_SENDER_EMAIL` | `--set-env-vars` | From address for outbound email |
35	| `BREVO_SENDER_NAME` | `--set-env-vars` | Display name for outbound email |
36	| `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` | `--set-env-vars` | Azure AD app client ID (not a secret — appears in OAuth traffic) |
37	| `AZURE_TENANT_ID` | `--set-env-vars` | Azure AD tenant ID |
38	| `PORT` | default `5000` | Express listen port |
39	| `NODE_OPTIONS` | `--set-env-vars` | Node flags for the Cloud Run container; currently `--max-old-space-size=896` to cap the V8 heap below the instance memory limit |
40	
41	**Critical:** Do not add secrets to `--set-env-vars` in `cloudbuild.yaml`. That flag replaces the full set of env vars on every deploy. Anything not listed will be wiped. Secrets belong in `--set-secrets`, which persists independently across deployments.
42	
43	## Adding a new secret
44	
45	```bash
46	echo "VALUE" | gcloud secrets create SECRET_NAME \
47	  --project=lithe-breaker-323913 --data-file=-
48	
49	gcloud secrets add-iam-policy-binding SECRET_NAME \
50	  --project=lithe-breaker-323913 \
51	  --member="serviceAccount:deployer@lithe-breaker-323913.iam.gserviceaccount.com" \
52	  --role="roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor"
53	```
54	
55	Then add to `cloudbuild.yaml` `--set-secrets` block:
56	```
57	SECRET_NAME=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/SECRET_NAME/versions/latest
58	```
59	
60	## App settings (persisted in DB)
61	
62	Two runtime settings live in the `app_settings` table and survive redeployments because they are in the database, not in environment variables. They are edited through the app UI (Editor → AI Settings, admin only).
63	
64	| Key | Value | Written by |
65	|-----|-------|------------|
66	| `ai-model` | OpenRouter model ID string | Admin via model picker |
67	| `section301_lookup` | JSON ~1 MB: HTS code → `{ list, rate, description }` | Admin via Section 301 CSV upload; legacy PDF endpoint still wired but UI-hidden |
68	| `section301_overrides` | JSON: `{ "NNNN.NN.NN": { list, rate, description? } }` — manual corrections consulted BEFORE parsed Section 301 data | Admin via Editor → Section 301 Overrides |
69	| `trump_2025_tariffs` | JSON: `[{ country, rate }, ...]` Trump 2025 reciprocal rates | Admin via Editor → Trump 2025 Tariffs |
70	
71	The MFN base-rate lookup is **not** stored in `app_settings` — it lives in `data/hts-rates.json` (bundled, ~1 MB, regenerated by `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` once a year when USITC publishes a new HTS revision). See `docs/architecture.md` § MFN base-rate lookup.
72

1	# Deployment
2	
3	## Pipeline overview
4	
5	```
6	git push → Cloud Build trigger
7	  → Step 1: docker build (node:lts-alpine → npm install → CMD yarn start:${NODE_ENV})
8	  → Step 2: docker push → Artifact Registry
9	  → Step 3: gcloud run deploy → Cloud Run
10	  → container start: models/db.js runs inline DB migrations
11	```
12	
13	No GitHub Actions. CI/CD is handled entirely by Google Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`).
14	
15	## Dockerfile
16	
17	- Base: `node:lts-alpine`
18	- Installs ImageMagick (for image processing endpoints)
19	- Copies source, runs `yarn install` (includes devDependencies for `cross-env` and `nodemon`)
20	- Exposes port 5000
21	- CMD: `yarn start:$NODE_ENV` — uses the start script matching the injected `NODE_ENV`
22	
23	The backend Docker image does **not** run tests at build time (unlike the frontend), and there is no separate GitHub Actions CI. Tests gate startup only for the `start:sandbox` and `start:production` scripts (which prefix `npm test &&`); `start:sandbox-albert` runs `nodemon` with no test gate. Run `npm test` locally before pushing. See [`development.md`](development.md).
24	
25	## Startup migrations
26	
27	There is no separate migration job. On **every container start**, `models/db.js` (loaded via `index.js`) runs a sequence of idempotent inline migrations against the database — `ALTER TABLE "RFQItem"/"Factory"/"users"/"customers"/"vendor"/"RFQVendor" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ...`, `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS app_settings`, and a `Factory.factory_country` backfill `UPDATE`. A schema change ships by adding an `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` statement to `models/db.js`; the new column is only realized in an environment once the new revision boots.
28	
29	**Mixed-case column identifiers MUST be double-quoted** in these statements. Sequelize quotes all identifiers; Postgres folds unquoted ones to lowercase. An unquoted `ADD COLUMN` created an orphan `rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots` that broke the RFQ grid (SYS-002, fixed in `a556d3f`). See AGENTS.md §14.
30	
31	## Image tag format
32	
33	```
34	{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/{PROJECT_ID}/{ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME}/{APP_ENV}/{APP_NAME}:{BUILD_ID}-{COMMIT_SHA}
35	```
36	
37	## Cloud Run service
38	
39	| Parameter | Value |
40	|-----------|-------|
41	| Service name | `popcre-albert-backend-sandbox` (sandbox-albert env) |
42	| GCP project | `lithe-breaker-323913` |
43	| Region | `us-central1` |
44	| Auth | `--no-allow-unauthenticated` (requests authenticated by BFF with OIDC token) |
45	| VPC connector | configured via `_VPC_CONNECTOR` trigger variable |
46	
47	Secrets are injected at deploy time via `--set-secrets`; non-secret config via `--set-env-vars`. See [`configuration.md`](configuration.md) for the full variable list.
48	
49	## Triggering a build
50	
51	The Cloud Build trigger `popcre-albert-backend-sandbox` is configured (in the Google Cloud Console) to fire on push to the `sandbox-albert` branch and auto-deploys the new revision. (The Cloud Run service for this env happens to share the same name.) Each environment has its own trigger with its own substitution variables.
52	
53	## Environments
54	
55	| Environment | Branch | Cloud Run service | Domain | Purpose |
56	|-------------|--------|-------------------|--------|---------|
57	| `sandbox-albert` | `sandbox-albert` | `popcre-albert-backend-sandbox` | `api.core.alsand.designflow.app` | Albert's personal dev/test |
58	| `sandbox` | `develop` | `popcre-core-sandbox` | `api.core.sandbox.designflow.app` | Shared staging |
59	| `production` | `main` | `popcre-core-prod` | `api.core.designflow.app` | Live production |
60	
61	**Note on domain names vs OIDC:** The domain column is for display/routing only. BFF services always call backends using the `.run.app` service URL as both the proxy target and OIDC token audience — not the custom domain. See the BFF repo `AGENTS.md` for the full explanation.
62	
63	## Rollback
64	
65	```bash
66	# List revisions
67	gcloud run revisions list \
68	  --service=popcre-albert-backend-sandbox \
69	  --region=us-central1 \
70	  --project=lithe-breaker-323913
71	
72	# Shift 100% traffic to a prior revision
73	gcloud run services update-traffic popcre-albert-backend-sandbox \
74	  --region=us-central1 \
75	  --project=lithe-breaker-323913 \
76	  --to-revisions=<REVISION_NAME>=100
77	```
78	
79	## Viewing build logs
80	
81	Cloud Build uses `logging: CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY`. Retrieve logs via Cloud Logging:
82	
83	```bash
84	# Find recent build IDs
85	gcloud logging read "resource.type=build" \
86	  --project=lithe-breaker-323913 --limit=5 --format=json
87	
88	# Stream full log for a specific build
89	gcloud logging read "resource.labels.build_id=<BUILD_ID>" \
90	  --project=lithe-breaker-323913 --limit=200 \
91	  --format="value(textPayload)"
92	```
93	
94	## Service account requirements
95	
96	The Cloud Run service account `deployer@lithe-breaker-323913.iam.gserviceaccount.com` must have `roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor` on every secret listed in `--set-secrets`. Granting access to a new secret is part of the [secret creation procedure](configuration.md#adding-a-new-secret).

100	## 6. Decision tree
101	
102	**Add a new API endpoint:**
103	1. Choose the right router (`lib.router.js` for RFQ/product/vendor; a named router for anything else)
104	2. Add a thin handler in `controllers/`; keep business logic in `services/` or `models/lib.model.js`
105	3. Gate with `authRole([...roles])` from `helpers/auth.js`
106	4. Add a Jest test in `tests/unit/`
107	5. Document in `docs/architecture.md` § Routes
108	
109	**Add a column to a database table:**
110	1. Add the field to the Sequelize model in `models/db/<Model>.js`
111	2. Add `sequelize.query(\`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"TableName" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "col_name" TYPE\`).catch(...)` inside `initialize()` in `models/db.js`. **If the column name is mixed-case (e.g. `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`), it MUST be double-quoted in the ALTER TABLE.** Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so an unquoted mixed-case name is folded to lowercase by Postgres and Sequelize never finds it — `findAll` then throws and the grid breaks (see §14, SYS-002).
112	3. Add a backfill query in the same block if needed (idempotent — guard with `WHERE col IS NULL`)
113	4. Add a regression assertion in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`
114	5. **Do not skip step 2.** `sequelize.sync()` does NOT alter existing tables. Missing this step causes "column does not exist" crashes in production (see §14, SYS-030).
115	
116	**Add a new Sequelize model (new table):**
117	1. Create `models/db/<Model>.js` following the existing pattern
118	2. Register in `models/db/init-models.js`
119	3. Write a `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` migration in `models/db.js`
120	
121	**Change auth or role logic:**
122	1. Auth middleware: `helpers/auth.js` (`authRole`, `getUserInfo`)
123	2. Role names come from the JWT payload: `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor`
124	3. Context (per-request user without prop-drilling): `helpers/context.middleware.js`
125	4. Do not add route-level auth bypass — all protected routes must use `authRole`
126	
127	**Change admin impersonation:**
128	1. Endpoint: `controllers/admin.controller.js` `startImpersonation`, mounted by `routes/admin.router.js` at `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId`
129	2. Normal user rows resolve `:userId` against `users.id`
130	3. Visible factory rows send `{ targetType: 'factory' }` and resolve `:userId` against `Factory.id`; this is the primary Factories & Reps path because the frontend may not expose a master-detail expand button.
131	4. Nested rep rows, if shown, send `{ targetType: 'vendor' }` and resolve `:userId` against `vendor.vendor_id`
132	5. Vendor/factory impersonation tokens must include `role: 'vendor'`, `access`, `factory_id`, and `impersonated_by`. For visible factory rows, `factory_id` is `Factory.id` and `email` may be `null`; downstream vendor endpoints must not require `vendor_email`.
133	6. `Customer.getRFQStep` must fall back to the JWT `role` when no user/vendor row resolves, otherwise factory-row impersonation can crash Cloud Run with `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'level')`.
134	7. Add/update `tests/unit/admin.impersonation.test.js` and `tests/unit/rfqStep.vendor-token.test.js` when changing this flow
135	
136	**Add a persistent app setting (survives redeploy):**
137	1. Use `services/app-setting.service.js` (`get(key)` / `set(key, value)`)
138	2. The `app_settings` table is the canonical key/value store (see §8)
139	3. For data that is static per calendar year and the same for every customer, prefer a bundled JSON file over an admin-managed upload (`data/hts-rates.json` is the model)
140	
141	**Add or change a tariff lookup (Section 301 / MFN / Trump 2025):**
142	- Section 301: `helpers/section301.js` + `routes/admin.router.js`
143	- MFN base rate: `helpers/hts-rates.js` (read from `data/hts-rates.json`); re-run `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` yearly
144	- Trump 2025: `helpers/trump2025.js` + `app_settings['trump_2025_tariffs']`
145	
146	**Refresh the bundled HTS schedule:**
147	```bash
148	node scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js
149	```
150	Run once per year when USITC publishes a new revision. Commit the updated `data/hts-rates.json`.
151	
152	**Add an email notification:**
153	1. `helpers/brevo.js` — Brevo transactional email wrapper
154	2. `services/notification.service.js` — in-app notification store
155	3. `helpers/mention.js` — @mention pipeline (auto-invoked from `addComment`)
156	
157	---
158	
159	## 7. Task-to-file navigation map
160	
161	| Task | Files to touch | Files not to touch |
162	|---|---|---|
163	| Add an RFQ/vendor/product endpoint | `routes/lib.router.js`, `controllers/main.controller.js` or `services/` | Other routers unless the feature clearly belongs elsewhere |
164	| Add an admin endpoint | `routes/admin.router.js`, `controllers/admin.controller.js` | `lib.router.js` |
165	| Change admin impersonation | `controllers/admin.controller.js`, `models/lib.model.js` if vendor-token identity is affected, `routes/admin.router.js`, `tests/unit/admin.impersonation.test.js`, `tests/unit/rfqStep.vendor-token.test.js`, `docs/architecture.md` | `helpers/auth.js` unless role verification itself changes |
166	| Add/change a DB column | `models/db/<Model>.js`, `models/db.js` (migration), `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` | Any other file unless the column needs a new service method |
167	| Add a tariff helper | `helpers/<name>.js`, corresponding test in `tests/unit/` | Route files (add endpoint separately) |
168	| Change auth pipeline | `helpers/auth.js`, `index.js` (if the middleware swap changes) | Route files (auth is middleware, not per-route logic) |
169	| Change file storage behaviour | `helpers/digitalocean.js`, `routes/digitalocean.router.js` | `helpers/multer.js` (unless upload constraints change) |
170	| Factory bulk offer upload (template or import logic) | `helpers/rfqBulkImport.js`, `helpers/digitalocean.js` (`uploadBufferToSpaces`), `helpers/multer.js` (`multerMemory`), `controllers/main.controller.js` (`getRFQFactoryTemplate`, `importFactoryRFQ`), `routes/lib.router.js`, `models/lib.model.js` (`getRFQFactoryTemplate`, `importFactoryRFQ`, `describeImportRowError`) | `routes/admin.router.js`, `helpers/auth.js` |
171	| Add an AI model/provider | `helpers/openrouter.js` | Route files (proxy is transparent to the route) |
172	| Change the notification service | `services/notification.service.js`, `routes/notification.router.js` | `helpers/mention.js` (only if @mention pipeline changes) |
173	| Update Trump 2025 rates shape | `helpers/trump2025.js` (validation), `routes/admin.router.js`, `services/app-setting.service.js` | Frontend (consumes lookup endpoint only) |
174	| Change build or deploy | `cloudbuild.yaml`, `Dockerfile` | `config/env.js` (only if adding a new environment) |
175	| Add environment variable | `.env.example`, `docs/configuration.md`, the relevant `cloudbuild.yaml` `--set-env-vars` or `--set-secrets` block | Hardcode into source |
176	| Add a Jest test | `tests/unit/<feature>.test.js` | Any source file (tests are read-only against the source) |
177	
178	---
179	
180	## 8. Data model
181	
182	See `docs/architecture.md` for full Sequelize model details. Key entities:
183	
184	| Entity | Key fields | Where defined | Notes |
185	|---|---|---|---|
186	| `users` | `user_id`, `email`, `name`, `role`, `profile_photo` | `models/db/users.js` | `profile_photo` was added via safe migration (SYS-030 — see §14). Auto-provisioned on first Azure SSO login with `level: 'designer'`. |
187	| `Customer` | `customer_id`, `customer_name` | `models/db/Customer.js` | Confusingly used as the top-level namespace in `models/lib.model.js` |
188	| `Vendor` | `vendor_id`, `vendor_country`, `factory_id_fk`, `vendor_access` | `models/db/Vendor.js` | FK to `Factory`; `vendor_country` is the per-vendor country override. Rep impersonation resolves by `vendor_id` when the request body has `{ targetType: 'vendor' }`; visible factory-row impersonation resolves `Factory.id` when `targetType` is `factory` and can produce a vendor-scoped token with `email: null`. |
189	| `Factory` | `factory_id`, `factory_nickname`, `factory_country` | `models/db/Factory.js` | `factory_country` added May 2026 via safe migration; backfilled from most common `vendor_country` |
190	| `RFQVendor` | `RFQVendor_id`, `RFQitem_id_fk`, `vendor_id_fk`, `RFQVendor_amount`, `quote_date`, `req_status`, `requote_requested` | `models/db/RFQVendor.js` | One factory's quote on one RFQ item. `RFQVendor_id` is the `quote_id` the frontend sends. **`vendor_id_fk` stores `Factory.id`, not `Vendor.vendor_id`** (see §11). `requote_requested` (BOOLEAN, added 2026-06 via safe migration) flags a stale price for refresh; mapped in `helpers/utility.js` `remapRFQItem`. |
191	| `RFQItem` | `rfqItem_id`, `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation`, `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, `Price Code` | `models/db/RFQItem.js` | `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` stores the raw 3-term duty breakdown (e.g. `"5.3+25+10"`). `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` (TEXT/JSON) maps each `*_price_sales` colId → `{duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at}`, snapshotting the cost inputs when a "Price for Sales" cell was populated; mapped both directions in `helpers/utility.js` (`remapRFQItem`/`mapRFQItem`), mirroring `rfqItem_requested_price_cells`. |
192	| `app_settings` | `key`, `value` (VARCHAR, stores JSON) | `models/db/AppSetting.js` | Managed by `services/app-setting.service.js`; see §6 for key inventory |
193	| `user_notification` | `id`, `type`, `event`, `title`, `message`, `unread`, `user_id_fk` | `models/db/UserNotification.js` | Managed by `services/notification.service.js` |
194	
195	**`app_settings` key inventory:**
196	
197	| Key | Value shape | Written by |
198	|---|---|---|
199	| `ai-model` | OpenRouter model ID string | Admin → AI Settings |

1	# Development Guide
2	
3	## Prerequisites
4	
5	- **Node.js LTS** (match version in Dockerfile)
6	- A `.env.${NODE_ENV}` file — copy `.env.example` and fill in real values
7	
8	## Install
9	
10	```bash
11	npm install
12	```
13	
14	## Run locally
15	
16	```bash
17	npm run start:sandbox-albert   # NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert, nodemon, no test gate
18	```
19	
20	Requires `.env.sandbox-albert` with all variables. The server logs the port, env file path, and key config on startup.
21	
22	Other start scripts run `npm test` first and abort on failure:
23	```bash
24	npm start                       # NODE_ENV=localhost
25	npm run start:sandbox           # NODE_ENV=sandbox
26	npm run start:production        # NODE_ENV=production
27	```
28	
29	## Run tests
30	
31	```bash
32	npm test                                        # all suites (257 tests, ~1.5 s)
33	npm test -- --testPathPattern="section301"      # single suite
34	npx jest --watch                                # watch mode
35	```
36	
37	Tests live in `tests/unit/`. All external dependencies (axios, pdf-parse, pg) are mocked — no real DB or HTTP calls needed. **Run before every commit.** There is no GitHub Actions CI and the Docker image does not run tests at build time. The `start:sandbox` and `start:production` scripts prefix `npm test &&`, so a failing test aborts container startup for those envs; `start:sandbox-albert` runs `nodemon` directly with **no test gate**, so you must run `npm test` yourself before pushing `sandbox-albert`.
38	
39	There are also integration/e2e test scripts (`npm run test:e2e`, `npm run test:e2e:ui`, `npm run test:e2e:report`) using Playwright, but these are not part of the CI gate.
40	
41	## Debug tips
42	
43	### Auth failures (403 / 401)
44	Check the JWT payload in DevTools: `JSON.parse(atob(token.split('.')[1]))`. Verify `role` claim matches the roles in `authRole(...)` for the failing route. A 403 means the token is invalid or missing; a 401 means the role is not in the allowed list.
45	
46	### Missing env vars at startup
47	`config/db.config.js` throws if any DB key is missing. `helpers/openrouter.js` `getKey()` throws synchronously if `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is absent. Both produce clear startup errors.
48	
49	### pdf-parse errors on Section 301 upload
50	`pdf-parse` is lazy-required — it only loads on first `parsePdf()` call. If the package is missing or broken, the error surfaces on the first upload, not on startup.
51	
52	### Context not populated
53	`contextMiddleware` runs before route handlers but after Express's built-in middleware. If `getContext()` returns null, the middleware is not mounted — check `index.js` middleware order.
54	
55	### Inline migration column "does not exist" / grid returns SYS-002
56	Inline migrations in `models/db.js` must **quote mixed-case identifiers**. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so a model column like `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` is queried case-sensitively. An unquoted `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` is folded to lowercase by Postgres, creating a column Sequelize can never find → `RFQItem.findAll` throws → `main.controller` returns SYS-002 and the RFQ grid shows "no rows to show". Always quote: `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots" TEXT`. Regression-guarded in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` (see AGENTS.md §14, SYS-002).
57

1	# MCP and External Data Integration
2	
3	This document describes how Designflow should expose company context, business data,
4	and images to other applications and AI coding/chat sessions.
5	
6	## Recommendation
7	
8	Build this as a hybrid:
9	
10	1. Keep Designflow's normal REST API as the source of truth for applications.
11	2. Add a thin MCP server for AI clients.
12	3. Have the MCP server call backend services or API-facing helpers instead of
13	   duplicating business logic.
14	
15	This gives regular applications predictable HTTP endpoints while giving AI tools a
16	standard way to ask questions like:
17	
18	- "How does RFQ pricing work here?"
19	- "Find the current item details and attachments for price code X."
20	- "What fields does the RFQ grid expose to vendors?"
21	- "Fetch images for this item so I can reason about packaging/artwork."
22	- "Explain the Designflow auth and deployment flow before changing code."
23	
24	## Why Not Only MCP?
25	
26	MCP is best for AI context and tool use. It is not a replacement for a public or
27	partner API:
28	
29	- Other applications usually want stable REST endpoints, pagination, filtering,
30	  webhooks, and service credentials.
31	- AI sessions want discoverable tools, resources, prompts, and concise context.
32	- Images and large binary assets should still be delivered through signed URLs,
33	  not inline MCP payloads.
34	
35	The MCP server should expose carefully scoped views of Designflow rather than the
36	entire database.
37	
38	## Recommended Shape
39	
40	```text
41	External apps
42	  -> Designflow REST API
43	  -> JWT or service token auth
44	  -> JSON data + signed image URLs
45	
46	AI clients / coding agents
47	  -> Designflow MCP server
48	  -> tools/resources/prompts
49	  -> backend services + docs + signed image URLs
50	
51	Designflow MCP server
52	  -> designflow-backend services/controllers
53	  -> Postgres / DigitalOcean Spaces / app docs
54	```
55	
56	## Where It Should Live
57	
58	Start in `designflow-backend`, because it already has:
59	
60	- JWT verification in `helpers/auth.js`
61	- request context in `helpers/context.middleware.js`
62	- Sequelize models and domain services
63	- DigitalOcean Spaces helpers for images
64	- docs and AGENTS guidance that AI sessions already need
65	
66	The first implementation can be a separate process under this repo, for example:
67	
68	```text
69	mcp/
70	  server.js
71	  tools/
72	  resources/
73	  prompts/
74	```
75	
76	Run it as its own Cloud Run service when remote AI clients need access. For local
77	developer use, also support stdio transport so desktop AI tools can launch it.
78	
79	## Transport
80	
81	Use Streamable HTTP for a remote service. Use stdio for local/private developer
82	sessions.
83	
84	The official MCP TypeScript SDK supports servers with tools/resources/prompts and
85	Streamable HTTP or stdio transports. Keep the MCP runtime thin: transport handling
86	belongs there; business logic stays in existing backend modules.
87	
88	## Authentication
89	
90	Do not make this an unauthenticated knowledge hose.
91	
92	Use one of these auth modes:
93	
94	- User-scoped: pass the user's existing Designflow JWT and apply the same role
95	  limits as backend routes.
96	- Service-scoped: use a dedicated service identity for trusted internal tools.
97	  This should be read-only at first and should log every call.
98	
99	Initial MCP tools should be read-only. Write tools can come later, but only after
100	we add explicit auditing, role checks, and narrow schemas.
101	
102	## First MCP Resources
103	
104	Resources are good for durable context that AI sessions read before coding.
105	
106	Suggested resources:
107	
108	| Resource | Purpose | Source |
109	|---|---|---|
110	| `designflow://docs/backend-agents` | Backend developer guide | `AGENTS.md` |
111	| `designflow://docs/backend-architecture` | Route/module architecture | `docs/architecture.md` |
112	| `designflow://docs/frontend-architecture` | Frontend structure | sibling frontend docs |
113	| `designflow://schemas/rfq-item` | RFQ item fields and meanings | Sequelize model + curated notes |
114	| `designflow://schemas/item-attachment` | Attachment/image field meanings | Sequelize model + DigitalOcean helper |
115	| `designflow://company/process/rfq` | Company RFQ process summary | curated internal doc |
116	| `designflow://company/process/sample-tracking` | Sample tracking process summary | curated internal doc |
117	
118	Avoid dumping raw model files into MCP as the main experience. Curated resources
119	are better for AI because they preserve company language, edge cases, and "why"
120	behind the code.
121	
122	## First MCP Tools
123	
124	Tools are good for current data lookups.
125	
126	Start with read-only tools:
127	
128	| Tool | Input | Output |
129	|---|---|---|
130	| `search_items` | text query, status filters, limit | item ids, price codes, descriptions, customer, image count |
131	| `get_item` | item id or price code | item header/detail, vendors, attachments, signed image URLs |
132	| `get_item_images` | item id or attachment ids | signed URLs and metadata |
133	| `search_rfq_items` | query, merch group, customer, status, limit | RFQ rows summarized for AI |
134	| `get_rfq_item` | RFQ item id or price code | RFQ item, vendor quotes, status, history summary |
135	| `get_grid_layout` | grid name and role | columns visible/editable by that role |
136	| `search_factories` | text query, country/group filters | factory ids, names, country, active state |
137	| `get_company_context` | topic enum | curated explanation of workflows and code ownership |
138	
139	Each tool should return compact JSON. For huge grids, return summaries and include
140	pagination cursors.
141	
142	## First MCP Prompts
143	
144	Prompts help AI sessions ask good internal questions consistently.
145	
146	Suggested prompts:
147	
148	- `prepare_designflow_code_change`: loads backend/frontend architecture, route
149	  ownership, auth rules, and the relevant process note.
150	- `investigate_rfq_behavior`: asks for price code or RFQ item id, then gathers
151	  item, vendor, quote, grid-layout, and audit context.
152	- `inspect_item_images`: gathers item attachments and signed URLs, then asks the
153	  AI to reason from both data and images.
154	
155	## REST API Additions
156	
157	Before or alongside MCP, add a small external read API:
158	
159	```text
160	GET /api/external/items/search?q=&limit=&cursor=
161	GET /api/external/items/:id
162	GET /api/external/items/:id/images
163	GET /api/external/rfq-items/search?q=&status=&limit=&cursor=
164	GET /api/external/rfq-items/:id
165	GET /api/external/grid-layouts/:name?role=
166	```
167	
168	These endpoints should use stable DTOs instead of returning raw Sequelize rows.
169	That protects external callers from internal column churn and lets MCP reuse the
170	same response shape.
171	
172	## Image Handling
173	
174	Return image metadata plus signed URLs:
175	
176	```json
177	{
178	  "id": "attachment-uuid",
179	  "kind": "product-photo",
180	  "fileName": "abc.png",
181	  "contentType": "image/png",
182	  "url": "https://...",
183	  "expiresAt": "2026-06-08T20:00:00.000Z"
184	}
185	```
186	
187	Do not stream large image bytes through MCP unless a client specifically requires
188	that format. Signed URLs are easier to cache, inspect, revoke, and audit.
189	
190	## Security Defaults
191	
192	- Read-only first.
193	- Return least-privilege data based on role.
194	- Redact vendor/customer-sensitive fields unless explicitly needed.
195	- Log tool name, caller identity, params summary, result count, and duration.
196	- Add rate limits for search tools.
197	- Avoid broad SQL-like filters from clients; use typed filters.
198	- Never expose raw database credentials or env vars through resources.
199	
200	## Implementation Phases
201	
202	### Phase 1: Internal AI Context
203	
204	- Add MCP resources for backend/frontend docs.
205	- Add `get_company_context`.
206	- Add `get_grid_layout`.
207	- Keep everything read-only.
208	
209	### Phase 2: Data and Images
210	
211	- Add stable external DTO service methods.
212	- Add `search_items`, `get_item`, `get_item_images`.
213	- Return signed URLs for image access.
214	
215	### Phase 3: RFQ and Workflow Intelligence
216	
217	- Add `search_rfq_items` and `get_rfq_item`.
218	- Include quote status, vendor visibility rules, and audit summaries.
219	- Add prompts for RFQ investigations.
220	
221	### Phase 4: Partner/API Hardening
222	
223	- Add `/api/external/*` routes.
224	- Add service-token auth and rate limiting.
225	- Publish an OpenAPI spec.
226	- Add webhook events only after consumers exist.
227	
228	## Testing
229	
230	Add tests at three layers:
231	
232	- unit tests for DTO builders and auth filtering
233	- MCP tool tests with mocked service calls
234	- integration tests for `/api/external/*` routes once added
235	
236	For image tools, assert that responses contain signed URLs and metadata, not raw
237	binary payloads.
238	
239	## Open Decisions
240	
241	- Which external applications need access first?
242	- Should MCP callers act as real users or as a single internal service account?
243	- Which data is allowed to leave Designflow for AI clients?
244	- How long should signed image URLs live?
245	- Do we need customer/vendor-specific data walls for AI sessions?
246	
247

206	## 9. Container inventory

224	## 10. What to ignore
225	
226	Do not index or deeply analyze these paths:
227	
228	```
229	node_modules/
230	coverage/
231	data/hts-rates.json   ← data, not code; ~1 MB; AI context budget wasted here
232	```
233	
234	The `data/hts-rates.json` file is a generated snapshot. When you need to understand its shape, read the first 10 lines or the `helpers/hts-rates.js` comments — not the full file.
235	
236	---
237	
238	## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions
239	
240	### `lib.router.js` and `lib.model.js` are giant files
241	
242	**Looks like:** Poor modularization — 150+ endpoints in one file; `lib.model.js` is ~3000 lines.
243	
244	**Actually:** Organic legacy growth. There is no formal split planned.
245	
246	**Do not change because:** Splitting would touch every import and require a coordinated, carefully tested migration. The API surface is still in flux.
247	
248	---
249	
250	### `models/lib.model.js` — namespace is called `Customer` but contains everything
251	
252	**Looks like:** Misnamed — the file handles vendors, factories, RFQ items, std products, art pieces, etc.
253	
254	**Actually:** `Customer` was the first domain object. Everything else accreted around it.
255	
256	**Do not change because:** The namespace name is internal. Renaming would touch every call-site for no user-facing benefit.
257	
258	---
259	
260	### Migrations live in `models/db.js`, not a migrations folder
261	
262	**Looks like:** The wrong place for schema changes.
263	
264	**Actually:** There is no `sequelize-cli`. The team uses inline `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` queries because they are idempotent and self-applying on every deploy.
265	
266	**Pattern:**
267	```js
268	// Inside initialize() in models/db.js, after sequelize.sync():
269	sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"TableName" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "col_name" TYPE`)
270	  .catch(err => console.warn('Migration <name> skipped:', err.message));
271	```
272	
273	`${schema}` resolves to `"designflow".` in prod (or empty in test envs). Always add a matching assertion to `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.
274	
275	**Quote any mixed-case column identifier** (`"rfqItem_col"`), not just the table name. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so an unquoted mixed-case column is folded to lowercase by Postgres, producing an orphan column Sequelize can never query (this caused SYS-002 — see §14). Existing migrations already do this (e.g. `"rfqItem_duty_rate_equation"`, `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"`). All-lowercase names (e.g. `factory_country`, `profile_photo`) don't need quoting, but quoting them anyway is harmless and consistent.
276	
277	---
278	
279	### `pdf-parse` is lazy-required in `helpers/section301.js`
280	
281	**Looks like:** Sloppy — package imported inside a function, not at module top.
282	
283	**Actually:** `pdf-parse@1.1.1` has known load-time issues on certain Node versions; lazy-require defers the cost to the first PDF upload. v2.x is broken in Node — stay pinned to `1.1.1`.
284	
285	**Do not change because:** Eager-requiring it can crash startup.
286	
287	---
288	
289	### `index.js` swaps `x-user-authorization` → `authorization`
290	
291	**Looks like:** Odd first middleware that overwrites a standard header.
292	
293	**Actually:** The BFF puts the OIDC service-to-service token in `Authorization` (so Cloud Run can read it) and the user JWT in `X-User-Authorization`. Cloud Run strips the OIDC token. This swap restores the user JWT to where `helpers/auth.js` expects it. **Without this swap, no route handler sees the user.**
294	
295	**Do not change because:** Removing it breaks all authenticated routes.
296	
297	---
298	
299	### `cross-env NODE_ENV=...` in every start script
300	
301	**Looks like:** Redundant `package.json` scripts for each environment.
302	
303	**Actually:** Each script loads a different `.env.<env-name>` via `config/env.js`. Adding a new environment = new script + new `.env.<env-name>` file.
304	
305	---
306	
307	### `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` not in `--set-env-vars` in `cloudbuild.yaml`
308	
309	**Looks like:** Missing configuration.
310	
311	**Actually:** `--set-env-vars` in Cloud Build is a **full replacement** — anything not listed is wiped on next deploy. API keys and secrets belong in `--set-secrets` (Secret Manager refs), which persist independently. See §13 and §14.
312	
313	---
314	
315	### DeepSeek direct API alongside OpenRouter for AI classification
316	
317	**Looks like:** Two separate AI client files (`helpers/openrouter.js` and `helpers/deepseek.js`) that do similar things — redundant.
318	
319	**Actually:** DeepSeek is available via OpenRouter, but routing directly to `api.deepseek.com` is cheaper (no OpenRouter markup) and exposes the `thinking` parameter directly. The `reasoning_effort` field (`'none'|'high'|'max'`) maps to DeepSeek's `thinking: { type, budget_tokens }` internally; OpenRouter may not expose this parameter.
320	
321	**How routing works:** `/api/openrouter/chat` checks the `api` field in the request body:
322	- `api = 'deepseek-direct'` → `helpers/deepseek.js`
323	- `api` omitted + model starts with `deepseek/` + `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` is set → auto-routes direct
324	- Everything else → `helpers/openrouter.js`
325	
326	**`DEEPSEEK_DIRECT_MODELS`** in `routes/openrouter.router.js` is a hardcoded model list that mirrors OpenRouter's `/models` response shape. The frontend reads it at `GET /api/openrouter/models?api=deepseek-direct`. Includes `prompt_cache` pricing (~10% of input price) for accurate per-turn cost tracking.
327	
328	**Do not change because:** DeepSeek's thinking-mode pricing (~$0.14/1M input, ~$0.014/1M cache hit) is meaningfully cheaper than going through OpenRouter for high-volume classifications.
329	
330	---
331	
332	### `RFQVendor.vendor_id_fk` holds a `Factory.id`, not a `Vendor.vendor_id`
333	
334	**Looks like:** A foreign key to the `Vendor` table, given the column name.
335	
336	**Actually:** It stores `Factory.id`. A `Factory` has many `Vendor` contact rows; the quote belongs to the factory, not an individual contact. `Customer.reqVendor` resolves the factory id from the vendor's `factory_id_fk` before writing it (`resolvedFactoryId = vendor_info.factory_id_fk`). The vendor JWT also carries `factory_id` (signed in `makeSingleToken` and the login path), so the two line up for the ownership guard below.
337	
338	**Do not change because:** Renaming the column or "fixing" it to a real vendor id would break every quote lookup, the chosen-vendor logic, and `emailVendorsForFactory`.
339	
340	---
341	
342	### Vendor quote ownership guard fails open
343	
344	**Looks like:** `helpers/quote-access.js` `vendorMayModifyQuote()` returns `true` in several cases instead of strictly denying — looks like a leaky check.
345	
346	**Actually:** `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) is a sequential integer, so a malicious vendor could enumerate other factories' quotes and POST `updateVendor` / `keepPrice` against them (IDOR). The guard blocks the one real case — a `vendor` whose token `factory_id` differs from the quote's `vendor_id_fk` — and deliberately **fails open** for internal roles, vendors with no `factory_id` on their token (legacy records), and quote rows with no `vendor_id_fk`. The intent is to close the enumeration hole without ever locking out a legitimate user (notably the invite-an-individual-vendor-to-one-item flow, where the invited factory owns its own quote row).
347	
348	**Do not change because:** Making it fail closed would block legacy vendors whose records were never linked to a factory, and `requestRequote` already hard-gates to internal roles separately. If you tighten it, backfill `factory_id` on all vendor records first.
349	
350	---
351	
352	### Trump 2025 `normalizeCountry` alias table is hand-curated
353	
354	**Looks like:** Fragile string matching.
355	
356	**Actually:** The alias table (`"PRC"` → `"china"`, `"Peoples Republic of China"` → `"china"`, etc.) is intentionally small and hand-curated. Admins should add factory countries under the name they use in their FOB Country master list to avoid alias gaps.
357	
358	---
359	
360	## 12. Environment and credentials
361	
362	See `docs/configuration.md` for the full variable list. Key variables:
363	
364	| Variable | Purpose | Where it lives |
365	|---|---|---|
366	| `DATABASE_URL` / `DB_*` | PostgreSQL connection | `.env.<env>` / Cloud Run secrets |
367	| `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` / `JWT_PRIVATE_KEY` | RS256 signing | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` |
368	| `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` / `AZURE_TENANT_ID` | Azure JWKS verification | `.env.<env>` / `--set-env-vars` |
369	| `SCHEMA` | PostgreSQL schema name (default: `designflow`) | `.env.<env>` |
370	| `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | AI proxy (OpenRouter) | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` |
371	| `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` | DeepSeek direct API | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` (`deepseek-api-key:latest`) |
372	| `DO_*` / `DIGITAL_OCEAN_*` | DigitalOcean Spaces | `.env.<env>` / secrets |
373	| `BREVO_API_KEY` | Transactional email | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` |
374	| `PORT` | Express listen port (default 5000) | `.env.<env>` / `--set-env-vars` |
375	| `NODE_ENV` | Selects `.env.<NODE_ENV>` file | `package.json` start script |
376	
377	**Never put secrets in `--set-env-vars`.** That flag is a full replacement on every deploy — any value not listed is deleted. Secrets go in `--set-secrets` (Cloud Run Secret Manager references), which persist independently.
378	
379	---
380	
381	## 13. Deployment
382	
383	See `docs/deployment.md` for the full pipeline. Summary:
384	
385	- **CI/CD:** Google Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`) — no GitHub Actions
386	- **Build:** Single-stage Node `Dockerfile`; image pushed to Google Artifact Registry
387	- **Runtime:** Google Cloud Run (managed, serverless, stateless)
388	- **Deploy trigger:** Cloud Build trigger on push to the relevant branch
389	- **Rollback:** Cloud Run revision traffic split via `gcloud run services update-traffic`
390	- **Env vars:** Plain vars in `--set-env-vars` (full replacement per deploy); secrets in `--set-secrets` (persistent)
391	- **Branch policy:** `sandbox-albert` → sandbox Cloud Run service. Never merge directly to `develop` — PRs only.
392	
393	---
394	
395	## 14. Critical incident log
396	
397	### 2026-05-29 — SYS-002: RFQ grid empty after unquoted `price_sales_snapshots` migration
398	
399	**What happened:** The RFQ grid showed "no rows to show" with a "Something went wrong (SYS-002)" toast. Every RFQ load failed.
400	
401	**Root cause:** The `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` migration in `models/db.js` was written with an **unquoted** identifier (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`). Postgres folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase, so it created `rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots`. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so `RFQItem.findAll` queried the case-sensitive `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"` that never existed → `findAll` threw → `Customer.getAll` propagated the error to `controllers/main.controller.js` `findAll`, which returned `sendError(res, err, 'SYS-002')` → empty grid. Every other migration in the file already quoted its identifier; this was the lone exception. The shipped unit test had asserted the *unquoted* form, so CI green-lit the bug.
402	
403	**Recovery:** Quoted the identifier (commit `a556d3f` → `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots" TEXT`) and added `ALTER TABLE "RFQItem" DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS "rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots"` (commit `476e453`) to remove the empty lowercase orphan column. Corrected `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` to require the quoted form and added guards: fail on the unquoted form, assert the orphan DROP exists, assert the correct mixed-case column is never dropped.
404	
405	**Rule:** Inline `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migrations in `models/db.js` **must quote any mixed-case identifier**. Sequelize quotes identifiers; Postgres folds unquoted ones to lowercase, creating an orphan column Sequelize can never find. See §6 and §11.
406	
407	---
408	
409	### 2026 — SYS-030: `profile_photo` column missing in production, login crashes
410	
411	**What happened:** `profile_photo` was added to the `users` Sequelize model (`models/db/users.js`) in commit `53cc531` without adding the matching `ALTER TABLE` migration in `models/db.js`. Every `sql.users.findOne()` in production failed with `column "profile_photo" does not exist`, crashing login.
412	
413	**Root cause:** Sequelize `.sync()` does not alter existing tables. Adding a field to a model without a corresponding migration is a silent no-op in dev (if the dev DB already has the column) but fails in prod.
414	
415	**Recovery:** Added the `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migration to `models/db.js` and deployed.
416	
417	**Rule:** Whenever you add a field to a Sequelize model in `models/db/*.js`, you **must** also add `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` in `models/db.js`. Add an assertion to `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.
418	
419	---
420	
421	### 2026 — SSO 401: BFF OIDC audience mismatch (recurring — at least 3 times)
422	
423	**What happened:** `POST /auth/azure` returned 401. Cloud Run logged: `The request was not authorized to invoke this service. The access token could not be verified.`
424	
425	**Root cause:** The BFF uses `CORE_BACKEND_URL` as both the proxy target and the OIDC token `aud` claim. Cloud Run validates `aud` against the service's `.run.app` URL. Custom domains route traffic correctly via Cloud Run domain mapping **but do not work as OIDC audiences**. When `CORE_BACKEND_URL` was set to a custom domain, every OIDC token was rejected.
426	
427	**Recovery:** Update `CORE_BACKEND_URL` (and `ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL`, etc.) on the BFF Cloud Run service config and `designflow-bff/env/.env.*` to the `.run.app` service URL.
428	
429	**Triage commands:**
430	```bash
431	# Check current BFF env vars on Cloud Run
432	gcloud run services describe designflow-bff --region=<REGION> --format='value(spec.template.spec.containers[0].env)'
433	
434	# Update CORE_BACKEND_URL
435	gcloud run services update designflow-bff --region=<REGION> \
436	  --update-env-vars CORE_BACKEND_URL=https://<service-name>-<hash>-<region>.a.run.app
437	```
438	
439	**Rule:** BFF `*_BACKEND_URL` values must **always** be `.run.app` service URLs, never custom domains. This applies to `CORE_BACKEND_URL`, `ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL`, and any future sibling service URL. This has been the root cause of SSO outages at least 3 times.
440	
441	---
442	
443	### 2026 — Secrets wiped after deploy: `--set-env-vars` is a full replacement
444	
445	**What happened:** A secret added manually to the Cloud Run service via the console disappeared after the next `cloudbuild.yaml` deploy.
446	
447	**Root cause:** `--set-env-vars` in `gcloud run services update` replaces the entire set of plain env vars on the service. Any variable not in the flag is deleted.
448	
449	**Recovery:** Move secrets to Secret Manager. Reference them in `cloudbuild.yaml` via `--set-secrets` (e.g. `--set-secrets=OPENROUTER_API_KEY=openrouter-api-key:latest`), which persists independently across deploys.
450	
451	**Rule:** Never put secrets in `--set-env-vars`. All credentials go through Secret Manager + `--set-secrets`. Only non-secret config goes in `--set-env-vars`, and every variable that must survive a deploy must be listed there on every deploy.
452	
453	---
454	
455	### 2026-06-08 — `designflow_dev` CP437 mojibake: scrambled Chinese/accents/©/smart-quotes
456	
457	**What happened:** RFQ grid column headers (and much more) rendered as garbage — `备注` showed as boxes/junk, `中国` as `Σ╕¡σ¢╜`, `Décor` as `D├⌐cor`, `Cortázar` as `Cort├ízar`, `©` as `┬⌐`, `'` as `ΓÇÖ`. The 12 visible RFQ header rows were the tip; a tripwire scan (looking for the box-drawing/garbage bytes that never occur in real data) flagged corruption in **thousands of additional rows across ~20+ text columns** — vendor/factory country, item descriptions, user last names, merch group, licensing feedback, vendor notes/addresses, audit-log history, email templates.
458	
459	**Root cause:** The entire `designflow_dev` schema was hand-loaded through a Windows console (cmd.exe) step whose code page is **CP437**, not UTF-8. Every non-ASCII byte sequence was reinterpreted through CP437 on the way in and stored that way — classic mojibake. This is a *data-at-rest* corruption from the load, **not** an application bug.
460	
461	**Scope / isolation:** Confined to the `designflow_dev` sandbox schema. The clean schemas (`designflow`, `designflow_sandbox`) had **zero** hits — the master data was never routed through the bad console step. The conservative tripwire only catches values containing box-drawing junk; a few mojibake classes (e.g. mangled smart-quotes `ΓÇÖ`) lack those bytes, so any count is a **floor, not a ceiling**.
462	
463	**Recovery:** The 12 RFQ header rows were repaired in place (live). A broader in-place repair of the remaining rows was scoped — re-encode each genuinely-garbled value (CP437→UTF-8 round-trip), skip already-clean values, and **leave image-link columns alone** (`RFQItem.rfqItem_picture` / `rfqItem_picturethumb`, `itemAttachment.attachment_link` / `item_attachment_fileName`): "correcting" a URL that points at a file actually stored under the garbled name would break the image. **Status of the bulk row repair is unconfirmed — verify with a fresh tripwire scan before assuming it ran** (a prior session that started it did not persist any of its work).
464	
465	**Prevention (in place):** `client_encoding: 'UTF8'` added to `dialectOptions` in both `designflow-backend/models/db.js` and `designflow-data-syncing/models/db.js`. This forces UTF-8 on the connection so the app can't introduce new mojibake; it does **not** retro-fix existing data.
466	
467	**Rule — re-seeding `designflow_dev`:** Never pipe a SQL/CSV dump into the database through `cmd.exe` (or any CP437 console). Use a UTF-8-safe path: `pg_dump`/`pg_restore` in custom/directory format, or `psql` with `\copy` and `client_encoding=UTF8` (e.g. `psql "...options=-c client_encoding=UTF8"` or `SET client_encoding TO 'UTF8';` before `\copy`). If you must use a console, run `chcp 65001` (UTF-8 code page) first and confirm the file itself is UTF-8. After any re-seed, run the tripwire scan to confirm zero garbage bytes. See also `designflow-data-syncing/README.md` → "Data encoding (UTF-8)".
468

## DESIGNFLOW BACKEND — GROUND-TRUTH FACTS REPORT

### 1. PROJECT SUMMARY

**What it is:** Designflow Backend is the main REST API for the Designflow PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) platform. It is a stateless Node.js + Express (4.22.1) service using CommonJS modules, backed by PostgreSQL via Sequelize ORM (6.37.3), with JWT (RS256) authentication on every request.

**Stack:**
- Node.js LTS (Alpine) + Express 4.22.1
- PostgreSQL + Sequelize 6.37.3 ORM
- JWT RS256 (verified per request via `helpers/auth.js`)
- DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible) for file storage
- OpenRouter + DeepSeek API for AI classification
- Brevo for transactional email
- Jest 29 unit tests (257 tests across 19 suites, hermetic, ~1.5 s runtime)

**Users:** Internal staff (admin, sourcing_manager, designer, sales, production) and factory vendors via role-based JWT claims.

**Key moving parts:**
- **RFQ module:** RequestForQuote grid, vendor quote management, factory bulk offer import (SYS-104/SYS-105, implemented but uncommitted per HANDOFF.md)
- **Product grid:** Item master, attachments, file storage
- **AI proxy:** OpenRouter (with native DeepSeek routing for cost optimization)
- **Auth:** Azure SSO login (→ `POST /auth/azure`), user auto-provisioning
- **Tariff lookups:** Section 301, MFN base rates, Trump 2025 reciprocal
- **Notifications:** In-app user notifications + Brevo email

---

### 2. REPO STRUCTURE

| Path | Classification | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `index.js` | **Project-owned** | Server entry; middleware (header swap for BFF), listen on port 5000 |
| `config/` | **Project-owned** | `env.js` (dotenv loader), `db.config.js` (PG params), `digitalocean.config.js`, `brevo.config.js` |
| `helpers/` | **Project-owned** | 25 files: auth, context, AI (openrouter/deepseek), tariff (section301, hts-rates, trump2025), factories, digitalocean, multer, error handling, mention (@-handle pipeline), rfqBulkImport (new, uncommitted), etc. |
| `routes/` | **Project-owned** | 18 router files including `lib.router.js` (150+ endpoints, 226 lines, legacy) and named routers (admin, auth, notification, user, openrouter, cross, comment, customer, grid, health, digitalocean, itemSize, itemDepth, std, role, autofill) |
| `controllers/` | **Project-owned** | Thin route handlers; delegate to `models/lib.model.js` or `services/` |
| `models/` | **Project-owned** | 65 Sequelize model files in `models/db/` (one per table: Factory, RFQItem, RFQVendor, users, vendor, Customer, etc.); `models/lib.model.js` (4,619 lines, legacy domain-logic namespace) |
| `services/` | **Project-owned** | Business logic layer (std.service, notification.service, app-setting.service, email.service) |
| `tests/unit/` | **Project-owned** | Jest unit test suites (19 files, 257 tests total) |
| `docs/` | **Project-owned** | `architecture.md`, `configuration.md`, `development.md`, `deployment.md`, `mcp-integration.md`, `api-reference.md` |
| `data/` | **Generated data** | `hts-rates.json` (~1 MB, USITC HTS schedule, ~13.6k codes; regenerated yearly by `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js`) |
| `scripts/` | **Project-owned** | `fetch-hts-rates.js` (one-shot yearly downloader from USITC) |
| `e2e/` | **Project-owned** | Playwright e2e tests (not part of CI gate) |
| `images/`, `thumbs/` | **Build artifacts** | Uploaded user files, thumbnails (ignored in .claudeignore, not committed) |
| `node_modules/` | **Vendor/framework** | Third-party packages (never edit) |
| `.git/` | **Version control** | Active branch: `sandbox-albert` (never merge or switch per CLAUDE.md) |

---

### 3. CUSTOM-CODE BOUNDARY

**Entire repo is custom code.** There is no committed vendor framework directory (unlike frontend's `src/@vex/`). Everything under root is fair game **except**:

- **`node_modules/`** — never edit
- **`models/db/*.js`** — Sequelize model files are effectively schema declarations. When adding a column, **must** add matching `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migration in `models/db.js` (inline, idempotent, applies on every deploy) — see §6 Decision Tree
- **`routes/lib.router.js`** (226 lines, 150+ endpoints) and **`models/lib.model.js`** (4,619 lines, ~3,000 lines of logic) — large legacy files; add surgically, do not refactor wholesale

---

### 4. BUILD/RUN/TEST/LINT

**Build command:** `npm install` (no special build step beyond dependency resolution)

**Start scripts** (from `package.json`):
```
npm run start:production       → npm test && cross-env NODE_ENV=production node index.js
npm run start:sandbox          → npm test && cross-env NODE_ENV=sandbox nodemon index.js
npm run start:sandbox-albert   → cross-env NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert nodemon index.js (NO test gate)
npm start                      → npm test && cross-env NODE_ENV=localhost nodemon index.js
```

**Node version:** LTS (Alpine image in Dockerfile)

**Test runner:** Jest 29.7.0
- **All tests:** `npm test` (257 tests, 19 suites, ~1.5 s, all hermetic with mocked deps)
- **Single suite:** `npm test -- --testPathPattern="<name>"`
- **Watch mode:** `npx jest --watch`

**E2E tests:** Playwright (not part of CI gate)
```
npm run test:e2e                 # run e2e suite
npm run test:e2e:ui             # interactive UI
npm run test:e2e:report         # show results
```

**Linting:** UNKNOWN (no mention of eslint/prettier in package.json)

**Pre-commit:** Run `npm test` locally before pushing `sandbox-albert` (start script does not auto-gate for `sandbox-albert`)

---

### 5. DEPLOYMENT

**Pipeline:**
```
git push → Cloud Build trigger (popcre-albert-backend-sandbox on sandbox-albert branch)
  → Step 1: docker build (node:lts-alpine, yarn install, CMD yarn start:${NODE_ENV})
  → Step 2: docker push → Google Artifact Registry
  → Step 3: gcloud run deploy → Cloud Run (popcre-albert-backend-sandbox, us-central1)
  → Container start: models/db.js runs inline DB migrations
```

**Dockerfile** (`Dockerfile`, single-stage):
- Base: `node:lts-alpine`
- Installs ImageMagick (for image processing)

### 11. INCIDENTS/QUIRKS/PENDING

**Documented critical incidents:**

| Incident | Date | Scope | Root cause | Resolution | Rule |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **SYS-002** | 2026-05-29 | RFQ grid empty ("no rows") | Unquoted mixed-case identifier `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` in migration → Postgres folded to lowercase → Sequelize never found the column → findAll threw | Quoted identifier; dropped lowercase orphan; fixed unit test | **Inline migrations MUST quote mixed-case identifiers.** Sequelize quotes all identifiers; Postgres folds unquoted ones to lowercase, creating an orphan column Sequelize can never find. |
| **SYS-030** | 2026 (unspecified) | Login crashes in production | `profile_photo` added to `users` model without matching `ALTER TABLE` migration in `models/db.js` → Sequelize.sync() does not alter existing tables → prod DB missing the column | Added `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migration to `models/db.js` | **Always add inline migration in `models/db.js` when adding a field to a Sequelize model.** Add regression assertion to `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`. |
| **SSO 401** | 2026 (recurring, 3+ times) | BFF OIDC authentication fails | BFF `CORE_BACKEND_URL` set to custom domain instead of `.run.app` service URL; Cloud Run validates `aud` claim against `.run.app`, not custom domains | Update BFF env var to `.run.app` service URL | **BFF `*_BACKEND_URL` values must ALWAYS be `.run.app` service URLs, never custom domains.** This applies to `CORE_BACKEND_URL`, `ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL`, etc. |
| **Secrets wiped** | 2026 | All secrets disappeared after deploy | `--set-env-vars` in `gcloud run services update` is a full replacement — anything not listed is deleted | Moved secrets to Secret Manager (`--set-secrets`), which persist independently | **Never put secrets in `--set-env-vars`.** All credentials → Secret Manager + `--set-secrets`. Only non-secret config → `--set-env-vars`, and must be listed on every deploy. |
| **CP437 mojibake** | 2026-06-08 | **designflow_dev** schema corrupted (~20+ text columns) | SQL/CSV dump piped through Windows `cmd.exe` (CP437 code page) → non-ASCII bytes reinterpreted → stored as garbage (`备注` → boxes, `中国` → `Σ╕¡σ¢╜`, etc.) | 12 RFQ header rows repaired in place; broader repair scoped (status unconfirmed — verify with tripwire scan); `client_encoding: 'UTF8'` added to dialectOptions to prevent future mojibake | **Never pipe SQL/CSV through `cmd.exe`.** Use `pg_dump`/`pg_restore` in custom format, or `psql` with `\copy` + `client_encoding=UTF8`. Run `chcp 65001` first if must use console. After re-seed, run tripwire scan for garbage bytes. |

**Intentional quirks & design decisions:**

1. **`lib.router.js` (226 lines) and `lib.model.js` (4,619 lines) are giant:** Organic legacy growth, not planned. Splitting would require coordinated migration. API surface still in flux.

2. **`models/lib.model.js` — namespace called `Customer` but contains everything:** Initial domain object; everything accreted around it. Internal name, no user-facing impact.

3. **Migrations in `models/db.js`, not migrations folder:** No `sequelize-cli`. Idempotent `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` queries self-apply on every deploy. Pattern (with example):
   ```js
   sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"RFQItem" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots" TEXT`)
     .catch(err => console.warn('Migration <name> skipped:', err.message));
   ```
   `${schema}` resolves to `"designflow".` (with trailing dot) in prod or empty in test.

4. **`pdf-parse` lazy-required in `helpers/section301.js`:** Package has load-time issues on certain Node versions. Lazy-require defers cost to first PDF upload. Stay pinned to `1.1.1` (v2.x broken in Node).

5. **`index.js` swaps `x-user-authorization` → `authorization`:** BFF puts OIDC token in `Authorization`, user JWT in `X-User-Authorization`. Cloud Run strips OIDC token. Swap restores user JWT before routes run. **Removing this breaks all authenticated routes.**

6. **`cross-env NODE_ENV=...` in every start script:** Each script loads different `.env.<env-name>`. Adding new env = new script + new `.env.<env-name>` file.

7. **`OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in `--set-secrets`, not `--set-env-vars`:** `--set-env-vars` is full replacement (anything not listed is wiped). Secrets → `--set-secrets` (persist independently).

8. **DeepSeek direct API alongside OpenRouter:** Direct routing cheaper (no OpenRouter markup), exposes `thinking` parameter. Auto-routing: `api='deepseek-direct'` → direct; omitted + model starts `deepseek/` + `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` set → auto-route direct; else → OpenRouter. `DEEPSEEK_DIRECT_MODELS` in `routes/openrouter.router.js` mirrors OpenRouter shape with native pricing (~$0.14/1M input, ~$0.014/1M cache).

9. **`RFQVendor.vendor_id_fk` holds `Factory.id`, not `Vendor.vendor_id`:** Quote belongs to factory, not individual contact. Factory has many Vendor rows. `Customer.reqVendor` resolves factory ID before writing. Vendor JWT carries `factory_id`. **Do not rename or "fix" — breaks quote lookups and `emailVendorsForFactory`.**

10. **Vendor quote ownership guard fails open:** `helpers/quote-access.js` deliberately returns `true` in several cases to avoid locking out legitimate users (legacy vendors with no `factory_id`, invite-to-one-item flow). Real check: blocks `vendor` whose token `factory_id` differs from quote's `vendor_id_fk`. **Fails open by design — tightening would break legacy records.**

11. **Trump 2025 `normalizeCountry` alias table hand-curated:** Small, intentional. Admins should add factory countries under the name they use in FOB Country master to avoid gaps.
